description_long: This is a small, classical-style brick pumping station with a symmetrical rectangular footprint and a prominent gabled roof. The roof is covered with gray slate tiles and features a central raised section, giving the building a stately appearance. The front facade is defined by three large arched openings supported by columns, with a triangular pediment above, reminiscent of neoclassical architecture. The side walls have evenly spaced rectangular windows with white trim, and the brickwork is detailed with horizontal banding and pilasters. The structure sits on a slightly raised concrete base, and the entryway is recessed beneath the arches. The overall design suggests a robust, utilitarian facility with architectural embellishments typical of early 20th-century municipal infrastructure.
description: A small, neoclassical brick pumping station with a gabled slate roof, arched front entry, and detailed masonry. The building features prominent columns, white trim, and symmetrical windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing three arched entryways, columns, a triangular pediment, and two side windows.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated front view, still highlighting the arched entry and columns, with the right side window more visible.
description_view_2: Another rotated front view, with the left side window more visible and the roof's central ridge clearly seen.
description_view_3: Front view similar to view 0, but with slightly different lighting and perspective on the entry arches and windows.
